# Generated Files

This guide explains what `claude-remote-config` writes in a consuming project.

## Human-authored files

This file is created by `setup` when missing and should usually be committed:

```text
.claude-remote-config.yml
```

`.claude-remote-config.yml` selects the remote, ref, profile, params, included tags, and excluded rules.

## Lockfile

This file is generated by `init` and should usually be committed:

```text
.claude-remote-config.lock.yml
```

It records the requested ref, resolved commit, selected profile, memory sources, selected rules, and settings sources.

## Ignored generated output

These paths are generated and are normally ignored:

```text
.claude-remote-config/generated/
.claude-remote-config/cache/
.claude/rules/claude-remote-config/
.claude/settings.local.json
```

`.claude-remote-config/generated/` contains generated shared memory.

`.claude-remote-config/cache/` stores cached remote git repositories when plugin data points inside the project.

`.claude/rules/claude-remote-config/` contains generated Claude Code rule files.

`.claude/settings.local.json` contains generated local Claude Code settings.

The generated shared memory is supplied to Claude Code by the plugin's `SessionStart` hook. `setup` does not create or modify `.claude/CLAUDE.md`.

## `.gitignore`

`setup` and `init` add a `claude-remote-config` block to `.gitignore` unless `--no-gitignore` is used.

The block is idempotent. Running the command again does not duplicate it.
